$comment: ICM config template. Copy this whole skeleton dir, rename, and edit. `model`/`embed_model` choose the local Ollama models. `tools` picks which capabilities the MCP server exposes (kinds are fixed: verify | docs_search | kb_answer | generate_verify) and names/describes them for your domain. `verify` is your deterministic oracle — change it to your language's compiler/linter/test runner; {code} is substituted with the candidate source.
name: my-icm

tools.name: verify
tools.description: Verify source compiles/passes the oracle. Returns PASS or the exact diagnostics.

tools.name: docs_search
tools.description: Search the reference doc corpus. Returns the most relevant items.

tools.name: kb_answer
tools.description: Answer from the curated KB, falling back to docs when uncovered.

tools.name: generate
tools.description: Generate code for a task; return only oracle-verified output.

verify.$comment: EXAMPLE: Rust via cargo. Replace project_files + commands for your language. For Python you might use {'main.py':'{code}'} + check ['python','-m','py_compile','main.py'] + run ['python','main.py']. Drop `run`/`test` if you only need a compile check.
